mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate on equities and relaxes financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next month when the cut is part of an easing cycle rather than a response to inflation shock.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline after FEDFUNDS declines, or if the signal turns negative when CPIAUCSL is accelerating year over year.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-term threshold signals labor market deterioration and rising recession risk, which compresses expected earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if threshold-triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in most cases, or if PAYEMS continues accelerating while the signal remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in RSAFS and GDPC1 indicates broad nominal demand expansion that supports corporate revenue growth and raises S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in RSAFS and GDPC1 do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect and the interaction adds no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO together with stable or falling CPIAUCSL implies real output growth without inflation pressure, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises above its 3-month average while CPIAUCSL is flat or lower over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition is followed by flat or negative 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if rising CPIAUCSL systematically reverses the sign.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above a restrictive threshold, additional upward pressure on the policy rate tightens liquidity and raises equity discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS regimes do not show lower 5-day SPX returns than low-rate regimes, or if short-horizon returns remain positive after policy rates exceed the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies labor market stress that foreshadows weaker consumption and earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if either series alone fully accounts for the signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in HOUST signals stronger residential investment and downstream demand, which should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as activity broadens beyond housing.
failure_condition: Reject if rising HOUST is not followed by positive 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the relationship disappears when housing activity is clearly contracting.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rise in GDPC1 and RSAFS during a post-downturn rebound signals broad demand normalization, which should produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next week as earnings expectations reprice upward.
failure_condition: Reject if joint rebounds in GDPC1 and RSAFS do not precede positive 5-day SPX returns, or if the effect vanishes when PAYEMS is not also improving.
